title: Finding Missing Relationships
summary: Use LEFT JOIN plus a right-side IS NULL check to locate missing related records.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding LEFT JOIN and IS NULL
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, a LEFT JOIN is used to combine rows from two tables based on a related column between them. It returns all rows from the left table and the matched rows from the right table. If there is no match, the result is NULL on the side of the right table. This feature is particularly useful for identifying missing relationships.

For example, consider the `departments` and `courses` tables. To find departments with no courses, you can use a LEFT JOIN and check for NULL values in the `courses` table:

```sql
SELECT departments.name
FROM departments
LEFT JOIN courses ON departments.id = courses.department_id
WHERE courses.id IS NULL;
```

This query returns all department names that do not have any associated courses.

sketchBlocks.title: Using Anti-Join Patterns
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
An anti-join pattern is a technique used to find records in one table that do not have corresponding records in another table. This is achieved using a LEFT JOIN combined with a WHERE clause that checks for NULL values in the right table.

For instance, to find students who are not enrolled in any courses, you can use the following query:

```sql
SELECT students.name
FROM students
LEFT JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
WHERE enrollments.id IS NULL;
```

This query lists all students who do not have any entries in the `enrollments` table, effectively identifying students without course enrollments.

quizDraft.title: Find Departments with No Courses
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to find all departments that do not have any courses associated with them. Use a LEFT JOIN and check for NULL values in the courses table.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT departments.name
FROM departments
LEFT JOIN courses ON departments.id = courses.department_id
WHERE courses.id IS NULL;

quizDraft.title: Identify Students Without Enrollments
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to list all students who are not enrolled in any courses. Use a LEFT JOIN and check for NULL values in the enrollments table.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT students.name
FROM students
LEFT JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
WHERE enrollments.id IS NULL;

quizDraft.title: LEFT JOIN Characteristics
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following statements are true about LEFT JOIN?
options: LEFT JOIN returns only matched rows from both tables.
LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matched rows from the right table.
LEFT JOIN fills unmatched right table rows with NULLs.
LEFT JOIN excludes unmatched rows from the left table.

quizDraft.title: Anti-Join Pattern
quizDraft.prompt: What is the purpose of using an anti-join pattern in SQL?
options: To find matching rows between two tables.
To find rows in one table without corresponding rows in another table.
To update rows in a table based on another table.
To delete rows from a table.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what an anti-join pattern helps you find.

help.concept: An anti-join pattern is used to find rows in one table that do not have corresponding rows in another table.
help.hint_2: It finds rows in the left table without matches in the right table.

quizDraft.title: LEFT JOIN Purpose
quizDraft.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a LEFT JOIN in SQL?
options: To combine only matching rows from two tables.
To include all rows from the left table and matched rows from the right table.
To exclude unmatched rows from both tables.
To update rows in the left table based on the right table.
quizDraft.hint: Consider what a LEFT JOIN allows you to do with unmatched rows.

help.concept: LEFT JOIN allows you to include all rows from the left table, even if there are no matches in the right table.
help.hint_1: LEFT JOIN preserves all rows from the left table.
help.hint_2: It helps identify missing relationships by including unmatched rows.
